claim_shape: (Currently Amended) A digital circuit implementing a machine learning model inference engine for one or more models, the digital circuit comprising:a hub-and-spoke data transfer architecture for each of the one or more models, wherein each of the one or more models comprises a neural network having an input layer, one or more intermediate layers, and an output layer, wherein the input layer, the one or more intermediate layers, and the output layer are processed sequentially, a plurality of neurons, said plurality of neurons comprising at least a first neuron set and a second neuron set, wherein thea plurality of neurons of [[the]]a first neuron set are not directly connected to any of [[the]]a plurality of neurons of [[the]]a second neuron set; a plurality of hub nodes comprising at least a first hub node and a second hub node, wherein the plurality of neurons in the first neuron set are only connected to the first hub node, wherein the plurality of neurons in the second neuron set are only connected to the second hub node; wherein all data transfers between and among each of a first layer and a second layer take place hub-to-hub, such that the data transfers between the first layer and the second layer occur between the first hub node and the second hub node; wherein the first hub provides the first set of neurons with data needed by the first set of neurons to perform respective computations within the neural network; and wherein the second hub node provides the second set of neurons with data needed by the second set of neurons to perform respective computations within the neural network. Currently amended 3
remedy: Examiner's ground: Claims 2-43 are rejected under 35 U.S.C. 112(b) as being indefinite for failing to particularly point out and distinctly claim the subject matter which the inventor or a joint inventor regards as the invention.
